title: Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy
subtitle: A stewardship model for staffing, translation, coordination, and continuity
summary: A generalized public entry describing how a focused SignWriting infrastructure core and philanthropic support could stabilize existing tools, standards, data, and international access work.
synopsis: Grants are essential for research, but they often leave continuity, staffing, translation, and infrastructure follow-through underfunded. This entry generalizes the infrastructure-core idea: a small focused stewardship unit for standards, software, data, translation, access, and coordination, with philanthropy treated as a bridge that complements grants rather than replacing them.

slides.title: Why A Core
slides.summary: A small infrastructure core would make standards, software, data, translation, access, and coordination more stable.

image_prompts.concrete: Program brief slide showing a small infrastructure core coordinating standards, software, data, translation, access, and partners
image_prompts.abstract: A compact stewardship core stabilizing multiple infrastructure functions

slides.speech: The infrastructure-core idea is modest. A small focused unit could make standards, software, data, translation, access, and partner coordination more stable without pretending to solve every research or adoption question.
slides.notes: Keep the model small and credible.

slides.title: What The Core Does
slides.summary: The core handles functions that grants often depend on but do not reliably fund.

image_prompts.concrete: Function slide showing grant-funded research depending on maintenance, documentation, translation, coordination, and reusable assets
image_prompts.abstract: Research grants resting on a practical continuity layer

slides.speech: The core handles work that grants often depend on but do not reliably fund: maintenance, documentation, reusable materials, translation, outreach support, and coordination before and after formal projects.

slides.title: Why Philanthropy Fits
slides.summary: Philanthropy fits as a continuity pathway that complements grants rather than replacing research funding.

image_prompts.concrete: Funding model slide showing grants and philanthropy as complementary paths supporting a stewardship core
image_prompts.abstract: Two funding streams supporting one continuity structure

bullets: Gifts can support translation and access
Funding paths should reinforce each other
slides.speech: Philanthropy fits because continuity work does not always align with grant cycles. It should not replace research funding. It can complement grants by supporting staffing, translation, access, and coordination.

slides.title: Modest Promise
slides.summary: The first promise is not universal adoption; it is a more stable and usable base for research, education, preservation, and collaboration.

image_prompts.concrete: Modest promise slide showing stable base for research, education, preservation, and international collaboration without overclaiming outcomes
image_prompts.abstract: A stronger base supporting several practical future paths

bullets: A stronger base for existing work
More usable infrastructure for future research
slides.speech: The promise should stay modest. The infrastructure core does not promise universal adoption or instant maturity. It promises a stronger base for standards, software, data, translation, access, and international collaboration.
